notes: All packaged cases were executed against the documented GCE VM localhost proxy workflow.
All cases inherit the evaluator localhost-contract warning: solve URL is not HTTPS and response_content_type is null on the proxy path.
Non-green cases were rerun once when needed; repeated outcomes were used for classification.

cases.details: Correctness 1.0 with 2 proxy calls and 0 client errors. Baseline remained optimal on the VM localhost path.

Both attempts returned 200 completed and performed a single POST /customer, but verification correctness stayed 0.0 because no customer matched the expected name+email+phone selector. Snapshot evidence shows matching-name records with blank email/phone values, indicating the solve path created an incomplete customer payload rather than a verifier crash.
Correctness 1.0 with a single proxy write call and 0 client errors.

Both attempts crashed in verification before scoring: tripletex_read_helper raised ValueError because GET /project returned 400 for the fixture read query. This points to an invalid verification read shape/fields on the packaged fixture path, not a solve-endpoint contract failure.
Both attempts crashed in verification with ValueError: GET /ledger/voucher returned 400. The failure occurs in the verifier read query, so the packaged verification fixture is the blocking issue.
Both attempts crashed in verification with ValueError: GET /travelExpense returned 400. Repeated verifier-read failure indicates a packaged read/query issue rather than a transient VM problem.

Both attempts crashed in verification with ValueError: GET /order returned 400. The solve run did not yield machine-readable scoring output because the verifier read failed first, so this is classified as a packaged verification/read issue.

Both attempts crashed in verification with ValueError: GET /order returned 400 on the packaged read query. This matches the invoice fixture pattern and is classified as a verification-fixture blocker.

Both attempts crashed in verification with ValueError: GET /supplierInvoice returned 422. The fixture read query itself is the failing step, so this is blocked by packaged verification shape/endpoint expectations.

Both attempts returned HTTP 422 from /solve before any proxy call: unsupported file MIME type text/csv for the packaged bank reconciliation attachment. This is a deterministic runtime attachment-policy gap relative to the packaged case expectations.
